rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY JOHNS ET AL VS. JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiffs' Motion For Leave To Amend To File Second Amended Complaint For Fraud, Conspiracy To Commit Fraud, Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Fiduciary Duty/ Conversion And Declaratory Relief And Specific Performance Seeking Order Compelling Election Of Directors Of Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Related Matters
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 22, 2017, line 1. PLAINTIFFs JOHNNY JOHNS and PAMELA KELLY's Motion For Leave To Amend To File Second Amended Complaint For Fraud, Conspiracy To Commit Fraud, Declaratory Relief, Breach Of Fiduciary Duty/ Conversion And Declaratory Relief And Specific Performance Seeking Order Compelling Election Of Directors Of Jones Memorial Homes, Inc. And Related Matters Off Calendar. Improper notice under CCP 1005(b). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIS RIVERA VS. HAWK LING LOU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Hawk Lou, And Ketty Lou'S Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Plaintiff Maria Corixza Nunez, Sandra Tenorio, And Isidro Funez
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 22, 2017, line 2. Defendants Hawk Lou, And Ketty Lou'S Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Plaintiff Maria Corixza Nunez, Sandra Tenorio, And Isidro Funez DENIED. The Court adopts the findings and recommendations of the Pro Tem Judge on May 19, 2017. In summary: 1) Defendants failed to show that their need for personal financial information outweighs Plaintiffs' privacy interest; 2) The Parties' efforts to meet and confer were adequate; 3) Defendant's failure to follow SFLR 2.11 was not a fatal error requiring dismissal; 4) Defendants' amended pleadings were timely filed from the date of the continued hearing of April 28, 2017. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EMMA DIAMOS VS. CAPITAL ONE, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Capital One, N.A.S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, May 22, 2017, line 6. DEFENDANT CAPITAL ONE, N.A.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings DENIED as to the third cause of action, but GRANTED as to the fifth cause of action for elder abuse. Plaintiff has sufficiently stated a cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Plaintiff has not stated a cause of action for elder abuse. = (501/REQ)
